summary.en: Denver's creative soul, an industrial-chic neighborhood where former warehouses and factories have been reborn as a canvas for the city's most dynamic art scene.
Perched on a hill across from downtown, LoHi is trendy and scenic, known for its unbeatable skyline views, excellent restaurants, and a lively, youthful energy.
Eclectic, funky, and fiercely independent, this is where you go for treasure hunting in antique shops, browsing vintage clothing, and discovering authentic dive bars and music venues.
A historic, dense, and diverse neighborhood of contrasts, where stately 19th-century mansions stand alongside mid-century apartment buildings with a slightly bohemian, lived-in feel.
The vibrant heart of Denver's Hispanic culture and grassroots art scene, packed with the highest concentration of art galleries in the state and authentic Latin American food.
A charming, walkable main street with a relaxed, local, and artsy atmosphere, lined with a quirky collection of independent boutiques, galleries, and one-of-a-kind restaurants.

description.en: An intimate, no-frills taproom focusing on high-quality, small-batch beers that fosters a strong sense of community.
A legendary New York cocktail institution offering sophisticated, world-class cocktails in a stylish, salon-inspired setting inside The Ramble Hotel.
A standout fast-casual Mexican spot serving authentic dishes while providing culinary and business training to immigrant and refugee women.
A vibrant restaurant dedicated to the rich traditions of Mexican pozole and mezcal, with a signature dish that is a must-try.
A bustling, upscale food hall housing nearly a dozen local vendors, ideal for groups with options from pizza to oysters.
The heart of RiNo, this is an ever-changing outdoor gallery of world-class street art from local and international artists.
A restored 1880s brick foundry housing a curated collection of artisans, restaurants, and shops.
A surprisingly chic spot featuring full-motion racing simulators paired with an impressive cocktail program for a unique social experience.
A modern food hall built from shipping containers, boasting seven culinary concepts and a spectacular rooftop bar with amazing skyline views.
A cozy spot specializing in a wide array of mezcals and creative, high-quality Mexican cuisine.
A Denver institution housed in a 28-foot-tall milk can, serving creative, homemade ice cream flavors that are worth the long line.
This iconic pedestrian bridge connects LoHi to downtown and is the premier vantage point for the classic Denver skyline photograph.
A beautiful, historic Gothic Revival church that has been repurposed as a unique event space, hosting markets and concerts.
Wander the quieter side streets to discover the neighborhood's true character in the fascinating blend of historic brick homes and ultra-modern new builds.
A cozy, plant-filled vegan bar with a global vibe, offering creative cocktails and excellent vegan small plates.
A vibrant, vegetable-focused restaurant with a lively atmosphere and delicious Mediterranean-inspired pastas.
A beloved roving pop-up with a cult following for its hearty and flavorful vegan comfort food. Check social media for current location.
A treasure hunter's paradise packed with dozens of independent dealers selling everything from mid-century modern furniture to quirky collectibles.
A well-curated vintage clothing store packed with unique clothing, boots, and accessories from various decades.
A charming boutique specializing in high-quality, carefully selected vintage clothing and accessories for a more curated shopping experience.
A legendary Denver institution, this bustling, artsy vegetarian cafe serves a massive menu from breakfast to late-night with a full bar.
A small, focused sandwich shop dedicated to perfecting one thing: incredible, slow-roasted Italian porchetta.
A tiny but mighty breakfast and lunch spot with a cult following for its Chinese and Southeast Asian-inspired menu, featuring bacon fried rice and congee.
The beautifully preserved Victorian home of Titanic survivor Margaret 'Molly' Brown, offering a fascinating glimpse into the life of an iconic Denver resident.
A beautiful, sprawling urban park with a grand neoclassical pavilion, formal gardens, and a slightly spooky local lore as it was built on a former cemetery.
An elegant historic house museum that tells the story of two prominent early Denver families, providing insight into the city's development.
The iconic government building on the edge of the neighborhood, famous for its gold dome and the official 'One Mile Above Sea Level' marker on its steps.
A unique hybrid spot that is part hi-fi listening lounge, part record store, and part coffee shop and wine bar.
A no-frills, cash-only Denver icon serving some of the most authentic and beloved Mexican street food, including a legendary green chile-drenched breakfast burrito.

name.en: Gallery Hopping on Santa Fe Drive

description.en: The main street is lined with dozens of galleries; spend an afternoon wandering in and out to discover contemporary painting, sculpture, and traditional folk art.
Look for large-scale murals on the sides of buildings that celebrate the neighborhood's deep Hispanic roots and cultural heritage.
On the first Friday of the month, the neighborhood transforms into a massive, lively street party where galleries stay open late and food trucks line the streets.
A 'fast-slow food' spot with a beautiful patio, serving creative and healthy vegetable-forward dishes inspired by global cuisine.
A cozy and perpetually popular spot for soul-warming bowls of ramen, fantastic kimchi dumplings, and other Japanese-inspired dishes.
Hailed as possibly the cutest boutique in Denver, this unique shop sells a range of artisanal, all-natural, floral-infused foods and gifts.
Stroll down Tennyson between 38th and 46th Avenues and pop into the various small, independent art galleries that showcase local and regional artists.
The street is known for its collection of small, locally-owned shops where you'll find unique clothing, gifts, and home goods.

prompt.en: Find a wall with an interesting industrial texture—like corrugated metal, old brick, or peeling paint—and take a close-up shot.
Take a selfie where you blend in or interact with a piece of street art.
Find the most colorful mural you can and take a photo that captures its vibrant energy.
Photograph something that you feel represents the innovative and artistic spirit of RiNo.
Look for a tiny or hidden piece of street art—a small stencil, sticker, or tag—and capture it.
Capture the atmosphere of a local brewery or taproom, focusing on the community feel.
Take a photo that shows the contrast between RiNo's industrial past (old warehouses) and its creative present (new art or businesses).
Find a puddle, window, or shiny surface and capture a reflection of a nearby mural.
Take an artfully arranged photo of a delicious dish or craft drink you're enjoying in the neighborhood.
Use the shadows cast by buildings or fire escapes to create a dramatic, artistic photo.
Find a depiction of a rhino (the neighborhood's mascot) and take a creative photo of it.
Take a photo looking down one of the art-filled alleys, using perspective to create a sense of depth.
Find a plant or flower growing in an unexpected industrial place and photograph it.
Find a piece of street art with text or a message and capture it.
Zoom in on a tiny, interesting detail of a larger mural that others might miss.
Find a spot with a view of the downtown skyline and capture the cityscape.
Take a photo that shows a historic Victorian home right next to a sharp, modern building.
Photograph a cool-looking coffee, cocktail, or ice cream that captures the neighborhood's trendy vibe.
Take a photo from a pedestrian bridge, using its lines to frame the city or the river below.
Find an example of interesting modern architecture and take a photo focusing on its clean lines and angles.
Find an old brick building and take a close-up of a unique architectural detail, like a window frame or cornice.
Capture a photo that shows off the neighborhood's rooftop culture, whether it's a bar, a patio, or a garden.
Take a photo of an interesting staircase or front stoop on a residential street.
Take a selfie that embodies the 'effortlessly cool' vibe of LoHi.
Take a photo looking down a hill towards the rest of the city.
If you're here at dusk, capture the city lights as they begin to sparkle.
Find and photograph the most unique or colorful front door you can find on a residential street.
Capture the scene at a bustling outdoor patio or beer garden.
Take an abstract photo of the reflections in a modern glass building.
Find a bench or spot that looks like the perfect place to sit and watch the city.
Photograph the most interesting or bizarre item you find in a vintage or antique shop.
Capture the storefront of an independent shop with a unique or eccentric design.
Find and photograph a cool old neon or retro-style sign.
Take a selfie reflecting in a shop window, showing off your best treasure-hunting look.
Find an object that looks like it has a great story to tell and take a photo of it.
Photograph a collection of mismatched items in a shop window or on a shelf.
Capture an image that represents the gritty, authentic soul of a local dive bar (from the outside is fine!).
Discreetly capture a photo that showcases the unique and independent fashion sense of the neighborhood's locals.
Take a picture of a delicious, unpretentious meal or snack from a local spot.
Find something with a lot of texture from age—like worn leather, cracked paint, or faded fabric—and take a close-up shot.
Find a wall or telephone pole covered in flyers and posters for local bands and events and take a photo.
Photograph a sign or symbol that you feel represents the fiercely independent spirit of South Broadway.
Take a creative photo of an interesting display in a shop window.
Photograph something you'd consider a 'hidden treasure'—something awesome that others might overlook.
Take a selfie with the most unusual background you can find in the neighborhood.
Find a stately old mansion and take a photo that captures its historic grandeur.
Take a close-up shot of an interesting architectural detail on a historic building, like a gargoyle, stained glass window, or ornate carving.
Capture a peaceful moment in Cheesman Park, focusing on the nature or the grand pavilion.
Take a photo of a place that looks like it could be haunted, playing up the neighborhood's spooky lore.
Find a historical marker or plaque and take a photo of it.
Frame a shot of the State Capitol's golden dome between trees or buildings.
Take a selfie that captures the lived-in, slightly bohemian feel of the neighborhood.
Photograph a historic mansion and a mid-century apartment building in the same shot to show the neighborhood's architectural contrast.
Find an interesting pattern in a wrought iron fence or gate and take a creative photo of it.
Find a beautiful garden or collection of potted plants in front of a historic home.
Take a photo of a winding path in a park or a historic cobblestone walkway.
Find an old, majestic tree in the neighborhood and take a photo looking up through its branches.
Capture the atmosphere of a long-standing neighborhood cafe or restaurant.
Use late afternoon light to capture long shadows cast by the historic buildings.
Take a photo of your feet at a spot you believe is exactly one mile high.
Find a building or mural with bold, vibrant colors and make it the subject of your photo.
Take a photo of a piece of art or a mural that you feel celebrates the neighborhood's Hispanic culture.
Photograph a piece of art in an unexpected place, like on a utility box or a garage door.
Take a photo looking into an art gallery from the street.
Take a selfie with a piece of sculpture or public art.
Capture a photo of some authentic Latin American food from a local eatery.
Visit a gallery and (if photography is allowed) take a picture of the piece of art that speaks to you the most.
Take a photo that captures the lively energy of Santa Fe Drive.
Find and photograph a sign written in Spanish.
Photograph something that you think is a perfect example of creative expression in the neighborhood.
Find an interesting pattern in a mural, a tiled entryway, or a building facade and take a close-up shot.
Look for an open artist studio and (from a respectful distance) capture a photo that hints at the creative process inside.
Take a photo of a place that feels like a community gathering spot.
Find a visual representation of music in the neighborhood—a mural of a musician, a record store, or a venue.
Take a photo that captures the dominant color palette of the Art District.
Find the most charming or quirky independent boutique and capture its storefront.
Photograph a unique, locally-made product you find in one of the shops.
Take a photo that captures the relaxed, neighborhood-centric atmosphere of Tennyson Street.
Find a small piece of public art, like a sculpture or a small mural, and take a photo.
Capture a creative and beautifully arranged window display in one of the boutiques.
Take an appetizing photo of a meal or snack from one of the local, one-of-a-kind restaurants.
Take a selfie with the bustling, walkable main street in the background.
Find a beautiful floral arrangement, whether in a shop, a hanging basket, or a garden, and photograph it.
Find a cozy-looking corner in a cafe or on a patio that looks like the perfect place to relax.
Take a photo of a sign or detail that emphasizes the 'shop local' spirit of the neighborhood.
Photograph a piece of art through the window of one of the street's many galleries.
Capture an inviting doorway or entrance to a local shop or restaurant.
Take a photo looking down Tennyson Street, capturing the line of shops and trees.
Find an item that is clearly handmade or artisanal and take a close-up shot that shows its quality.
Photograph a detail that you think perfectly sums up the unique character of the Tennyson Street district.
